# Analog Devices Hiring Systems Co-Op: Wilmington, MA ($22-$41)

By P S Karthik

On: May 20, 2026 12:17 AM

Follow Us:

A career opportunity at Analog Devices offers aspiring engineers and technology professionals the chance to gain practical experience in a globally recognized semiconductor and technology company. The Systems Co-Op role in Wilmington, Massachusetts is designed for students and emerging professionals who want hands-on exposure to real-world system development, engineering collaboration, and innovative technologies. Candidates joining this program can strengthen technical expertise while working alongside experienced professionals in a dynamic and innovation-driven environment.

## Job Overview

| Field | Details |
| --- | --- |
| Company Name | Analog Devices |
| Role | Software and Systems Co-Op (Summer/Fall) |
| Qualification | Currently pursuing a Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Engineering, Computer Science, Electronics, or a related field |
| Job Location | Wilmington, MA, United States |
| Salary | $22–$41 per hour |
| Work Type | On-site / Hybrid (based on business requirements) |
| Job Type | Internship / Co-Op |
| Job Level | Entry Level |
| Industry | Semiconductor / Technology |

### Job Description

The Fab Digital Solutions team is seeking a motivated Software and Systems Co-Op (June – December) to support the Manufacturing Site IT team located at ADI’s Wilmington Site. This Co-Op will support a variety of needs including Application Development, System Administration, Data Visualization, and Equipment Automation.

The Fab Digital Solutions team develops and supports custom web applications to improve access to data, streamline operations, and improve communication throughout the manufacturing organization, and is looking for an intern with strong self-direction and great communication skills to assist with development, testing, implementation, and documentation.

Responsibilities include, but not limited to:

- Documenting existing libraries and applications
- Identifying opportunities for standardization and improvement of code base
- Assisting team with generating and reporting performance metrics
- Configuring and deploying systems, applications, and components to support the Manufacturing Organization
- Developing prototype applications based on expressed needs

Minimum Qualifications

- Pursuing a bachelor’s or master’s degree in Engineering, Computer Science, or Similar Field of Study
- Experience with software development (Web Development with HTML, CSS, JavaScript, C#, PHP)
- Experience with AI tools (Chatbots and Agents)
- Experience with Git Version Control tools

### Selection Process

- Application Submission
- Resume Screening
- Initial HR Review
- Technical Assessment (if applicable)
- Technical Interview Round
- Manager/Team Interview
- Final Selection and Offer Letter

### How to Apply

- Visit the official Analog Devices careers page
- Search for the “Systems Co-Op – Wilmington, MA” position
- Review the eligibility criteria and job requirements
- Prepare and update your resume with relevant skills and experience
- Complete the online application form
- Upload required documents and submit your application
- Monitor your email for interview and recruitment updates

Click To Apply Online

Predicts decentralized AI architectures will move from pilot to early commercial deployment in humanoid robotics by end of 2026, with local sensory compute handling reflexes and balance to free central processors for planning.
[...]
ADI's AI strategy is architecturally distinct from every other semiconductor company in the S&P 500. Rather than competing in the GPU/accelerator arms race dominated by Nvidia, or the edge inference market where Qualcomm and Intel jostle, ADI is building AI compute into the analog signal chain itself. The flagship technology — Analog AI Compute, expected to hit the market in late 2026 — performs neural network inference using the physical properties of analog circuits (resistance, capacitance, charge) rather than digital transistor logic. The result is inference at a fraction of the power consumption of conventional digital AI chips, targeting applications where milliwatt-level budgets are non-negotiable: industrial predictive maintenance sensors, implantable medical devices, and autonomous edge nodes. The Andromeda Platform, launched in 2025, is the software complement to this hardware vision. It enables cloud-to-edge AI training on raw, unprocessed sensor data — vibration signatures, acoustic patterns, electromagnetic waveforms — so that industrial machines can learn anomaly patterns without the latency and bandwidth cost of streaming raw data to the cloud. This is a direct play against the 'ship everything to the cloud' orthodoxy that still dominates most industrial IoT deployments. ADI's AI talent bench is anchored by Massimiliano Versace, VP of Emergent AI, who leads research into neuromorphic and in-memory compute architectures. His team's work on decentralized AI — where local circuits in robotic limbs handle reflexes and balance autonomously, freeing a central processor for higher-order planning — maps directly to the humanoid robotics opportunity that companies like Figure and Tesla are pursuing. The Flex Logix acquisition (November 2024) brought embedded FPGA IP and AI inference silicon expertise in-house, giving ADI reconfigurable compute blocks it can integrate alongside its analog front ends. The most immediate AI revenue driver, however, is not edge inference but data center power. ADI's Silent Switcher power regulators and high-performance power ICs are critical for the power delivery networks inside AI training and inference servers. The company's data center business grew approximately 50% in fiscal 2025 and accelerated further in Q1 FY2026. Analysts estimate the liquid-cooled AI server power management opportunity alone could represent a $2 billion incremental revenue stream for ADI by 2028. This makes ADI a lower-volatility AI proxy — you don't need to pick the winning GPU architecture when every GPU needs ADI's power management silicon to run.
